mnemonics: C-F-A-Q (Clarity, Functionality, Assurance, Quality) - Remember the pillars of software quality: Correctness, Functionality, Assurance, and Quality in use.
keyPoints: Software Quality Assurance (SQA) is a process that ensures software meets predefined quality standards.
Key quality attributes include correctness, reliability, usability, efficiency, maintainability, and portability.
Standards like ISO 9126 and models like CMMI provide frameworks for defining and measuring software quality.
commonMistakes: Equating a lack of bugs with high quality, ignoring non-functional requirements like usability and maintainability.
Focusing on testing as the sole method for quality assurance, instead of building quality in throughout the entire development process.
